links.label: weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver ... the thirty pieces of silver the price
cast them to the potter in the house of the LORD

name: Thirty pieces of silver
notes: Zechariah's prophecy of the shepherd's wage — thirty pieces of silver weighed out and cast unto the potter in the house of the LORD — is taken up by Matthew as fulfilled in Judas's betrayal money. The shared core is the exact sum (thirty pieces of silver), the casting down of the silver, and the potter, which in the gospel becomes the potter's field bought with the price of blood. Zechariah supplies the prophetic words and the disdained "goodly price"; Matthew supplies the narrative of Judas's remorse, the priests' refusal of blood money, and the purchase of the field. Matthew 27:9 names "Jeremy the prophet" though the quoted wording is Zechariah's; this is a recognized point careful readers weigh, since Jeremiah heads the prophetic collection and Jeremiah 19 and 32 themselves supply a potter and the buying of a field, so the citation stands under Jeremiah's name within that prophetic book. It is left as a matter for further study rather than treated as any flaw in the text.
